The collimated thermal neutron beam passed
through a 100-micron-thick Al window onto the target,
which was located in the center of the cylindrical
reaction chamber. The reaction chamber(350 mm diameter,
walls manufactured from 2 mm duraluminum) was filled
with isobutane up to a pressure of 4-5 Torr.

Double discrimination by the pulse shape and

Four cycles ( each of two weeks) of measurement were
performed. During each cycle, the angular and energy
distributions of the prompt fission neutrons were
measured in turn for the thermal-neutron-induced
fission of 235U and the spontaneous fission of 252Cf.
The 252Cf target was installed in the reaction chamber
at the same position as the 235U target.

- 16 MWPD of 3 mm thickness, 72x38 mm2 square,
installed in two arcing formations (8 MWPCs in each
formation, diametrically opposite to each other) in
the reaction chamber at a distance of 140 mm from the
chamber axis were used to determine the velocities of
the fission fragments and their directions of motion.
